claimPolicy.allowedClaim: gofly may claim governed RPC, rpc/grpc compatibility evidence, control-plane visibility, generated service integration, and coexistence rollback evidence when the listed gates pass.
claimPolicy.promotionPolicy: Tier 1 promotion stays blocked until one release train attaches RPC boundary evidence and selected RPC unary or stream benchmark rows move from report-only candidates to blocking budget coverage.
claimPolicy.rollbackPolicy: Keep Kitex or native gRPC-Go on latency-critical transport paths until benchmark, stream, resolver, balancer, deadline, retry, and release evidence all pass locally.

rows.forbiddenClaim: Kitex or gRPC-Go streaming transport parity
rows.rollbackPolicy: Keep mode-specific streams on Kitex or gRPC-Go until each stream benchmark and compatibility row passes for the adopter workload.

rows.forbiddenClaim: service-mesh or Kitex client-side routing parity
rows.rollbackPolicy: Keep previous discovery, service mesh, or client balancer routing active until resolver updates and health-aware policy evidence pass.

rows.forbiddenClaim: native gRPC-Go ecosystem parity beyond tested health, auth, tracing, retry, and deadline surfaces
rows.rollbackPolicy: Keep native gRPC-Go behavior when deadline taxonomy, retryable statuses, auth metadata, or tracing propagation differ from production samples.

rows.forbiddenClaim: blocking RPC latency or allocation budget coverage before bench-regression-check promotion
rows.rollbackPolicy: Keep latency-critical methods on Kitex or existing gRPC clients while RPC benchmark rows remain report-only candidates.

rows.forbiddenClaim: in-place migration without parallel registration and rollback routing
rows.rollbackPolicy: Keep original Kitex or go-zero services registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, discovery, or RPC evidence diverges.

transportBoundaries.keepWhen: the workload depends on Netpoll, TTHeader, Thrift, generated Kitex clients, or latency-critical transport behavior
transportBoundaries.goflyRole: REST ingress, generated service glue, governance rules, control-plane evidence, release gates, and non-hot-path RPC
transportBoundaries.rollbackOrEscalation: Route hot methods back to Kitex whenever benchmark, stream governance, or coexistence rollback evidence is incomplete.

transportBoundaries.keepWhen: the workload depends on native gRPC ecosystem behavior, generated clients, health, auth, tracing, retry service config, or streaming compatibility
transportBoundaries.goflyRole: rpc/grpc compatibility layer, governance interceptors, health/auth/tracing evidence, and generated control-plane integration
transportBoundaries.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable until compatibility tests and release evidence pass for the target workload.

transportBoundaries.keepWhen: the migration depends on existing zrpc discovery, timeout, retry, breaker, rate-limit, or generated service layout semantics
transportBoundaries.goflyRole: generated service migration, governance policy, REST/RPC shape comparison, discovery visibility, and rollback routing rehearsal
transportBoundaries.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the go-zero service registered and route traffic back when generated output, control-plane, or RPC evidence diverges.

promotionBlockers.requiredAction: Attach at least one completed release train with RPC boundary, required-check drift, release evidence, and benchmark evidence before Tier 1 promotion.
Promote selected RPC unary or stream rows into bench-regression-check blocking budget coverage only after trend confidence passes.
Reject release notes or docs that imply Kitex or gRPC-Go transport parity without transport-layer benchmark, protocol, streaming, and rollback evidence.

evidenceLinks.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep stream workloads on the existing RPC stack until mode-specific stream overhead evidence and rollback notes pass.
Keep service mesh or previous client routing active until resolver updates and balancer policies are rehearsed.
Keep existing client behavior when deadline, retryable status, auth, or unavailable mapping differs from sampled production behavior.

promotionPath.evidence: Unary, stream, resolver, balancer, deadline, retry, auth, tracing, and rollback rows are machine-checkable.
One release train must attach release evidence, required-check drift evidence, RPC boundary evidence, and benchmark evidence.
Selected RPC unary or stream rows must move into blocking allocation budget coverage before Tier 1 promotion.

fallbackMatrix.trigger: latency-critical workload, Netpoll, TTHeader, Thrift, generated Kitex client, or missing RPC budget promotion
fallbackMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep Kitex registered and route hot methods back until release-train and benchmark evidence both pass.

fallbackMatrix.trigger: native gRPC health, auth, tracing, retry service config, generated client, or streaming compatibility drift
fallbackMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep native gRPC-Go endpoints reachable until compatibility and release evidence pass for the adopter workload.

fallbackMatrix.trigger: zrpc discovery, timeout, retry, breaker, rate-limit, generated layout, or rollback routing evidence diverges
fallbackMatrix.rollbackAction: Keep the previous go-zero service registered and route traffic back while generated output or policy drift is fixed.

forbiddenClaims: blocking RPC latency budget before promotion
